title: MyCR full run 2026-07-09 01:25 CST
overview: 本次先在 mycr 仓库执行 git pull --ff-only，结果 already up to date；随后对 trpc-group/trpc-agent-go 做全量 review sweep。最终 open PR 为 55 个，最终 checks 快照为 26 个全绿、29 个非全绿。本轮严格执行 comment-only 要求，没有 LGTM、approve 或 merge。实际深审后只在 #2151 和 #2128 提交 COMMENT review：#2151 阻断点是 code review agent 示例的 diff 范围、shell 执行和本地 sandbox 默认安全边界；#2128 阻断点是 GORM memory backend 的 limit 检查顺序和软删除计数。其余 53 个 PR 按 draft/WIP、own PR、CI、既有 changes-requested、外部贡献者人工 LGTM、以及 comment-only 不合入策略分组归档，报告覆盖记录已覆盖全部 open PR。

commented.title: examples/code_review_agent: add sandboxed code review agent example
commented.author: XnLemon
commented.technical_background: The example adds an agent that fetches pull-request diffs, runs configurable checks, and can execute repository tests through a sandbox abstraction before producing review comments.
commented.problem: Three safety and coverage bugs make the example unsafe as a default reviewer: it only diffs the last commit, it builds a shell command from unchecked script path and arguments, and it silently falls back to host-local execution when no external sandbox is configured.
commented.necessity_assessment: These are blocking for an example that may be copied into real review automation, because they can miss earlier commits in a PR and can run untrusted contributor code on the reviewer machine.
commented.evidence_checked: Reviewed `examples/code_review_agent/main.go`, `sandbox/sandbox.go`, existing review comments, the current PR head, and the final checks snapshot.
commented.problem_framing: The PR changes a review workflow, so correctness depends on both diff scope and execution isolation; a passing test suite does not prove the example is safe to run on untrusted pull requests.
commented.approach: The implementation introduces a sandbox interface and a default local implementation, but the call sites need a fail-closed path and argv-based command execution rather than shell string construction.
commented.implementation_derivation: `git diff HEAD~1 HEAD` is derived from local commit history rather than the pull request merge base, and `ExecuteScript` formats `scriptPath` plus `args` into `sh -c`.
commented.solution_fit_assessment: The high-level example is useful, but these guardrails need to be fixed before the example can be treated as a safe reference implementation.
commented.alternative_designs: Use a merge-base or GitHub diff file for review scope; use `exec.CommandContext` with explicit argv for script execution; require an explicit unsafe-local flag or configured container sandbox before running tests.
commented.tradeoffs: Failing closed makes first-run setup slightly less convenient, but prevents a sample review agent from normalizing host execution of arbitrary PR tests.
commented.design_assessment: Needs changes before approval. The concerns are narrow and local to the example, but they affect the security boundary and review coverage of the new workflow.
commented.api_surface: No library API change, but the example CLI behavior and documented safe defaults should change.
commented.change_inventory: New PR fetcher, diff parser, policy detector, analyzer, comment reporter, and sandbox execution path under `examples/code_review_agent`.
commented.semantic_changes: Adds a runnable code-review example that can execute tests and post review output.
commented.module_impact: Limited to the example module, with safety implications for anyone copying the workflow.
commented.cross_module_impact: No direct runtime package impact, but it exercises repository tests across arbitrary target modules when enabled.
commented.behavior_impact: Without changes, multi-commit PRs may be only partially reviewed and contributor-controlled script arguments can affect host shell execution.
commented.reproduction_on_base: The problems are visible from the current implementation: `git diff HEAD~1 HEAD` cannot cover multi-commit PR history, and `sh -c` receives concatenated script path and arguments.
commented.issue_evidence_gaps: No additional maintainer context is needed for the command-injection and default-local-execution issues; the desired diff source may need maintainer preference.
commented.tests_docs: `go test ./...` passed in the example module. Existing tests do not cover multi-commit diff selection or unsafe local fallback behavior.
commented.outcome: Submitted a COMMENT review with three inline comments; no approval, LGTM, or merge action was taken.
commented.ci_state: All effective checks were green in the final snapshot.
commented.risk: Leaving the example as-is can train contributors to run untrusted review workloads on their host and can miss changed files outside the final commit.
commented.merge_policy: Comment-only run; external contributor PR also requires explicit maintainer LGTM before merge.

inline_comments.summary: `git diff HEAD~1 HEAD` only reviews the last commit; use the PR merge base/base ref or an explicit diff input.
Script path and args are concatenated into `sh -c`, creating a shell-injection path; execute with argv instead.
Defaulting to LocalSandbox runs untrusted PR tests on the host; fail closed or require an explicit unsafe-local mode.

commented.title: feat(memory/gorm): Add GORM-based memory service with multi-database support
commented.author: jrx2025
commented.technical_background: The memory service stores user-scoped memories through GORM and supports upsert, soft delete, query filtering, and an optional per-user memory limit across multiple SQL databases.
commented.problem: The memory-limit check runs before canonical memory ID lookup. At the configured limit, re-adding the same memory fails even though it should update the existing row, and soft-deleted rows still count against the active-memory limit.
commented.necessity_assessment: This blocks normal idempotent writes and replacement flows, which are core behaviors for a memory backend that deduplicates or updates memories by generated ID.
commented.evidence_checked: Reviewed `memory/gorm/service.go`, compared the limit semantics with the SQLite memory service, checked current review threads, and ran targeted module tests.
commented.problem_framing: The limit is meant to protect active memory cardinality, not reject updates to the same canonical memory or count rows that were explicitly soft deleted.
commented.approach: The implementation checks count first and only later computes the memory ID, so it cannot distinguish a new insert from an update to an existing memory.
commented.implementation_derivation: `AddMemory` calls `countUserMemories` before `generateMemoryID`, and `countUserMemories` queries by `user_id` without excluding soft-deleted records.
commented.solution_fit_assessment: The GORM backend structure is broadly aligned with the package, but the order of limit enforcement should match the memory identity semantics.
commented.alternative_designs: Generate the memory ID first, check whether that active row already exists, and count only active rows before rejecting a truly new insert at the limit.
commented.tradeoffs: Checking identity first adds one lookup but preserves idempotency and makes soft delete free capacity as users expect.
commented.design_assessment: Needs a focused fix before approval. The issue is local to `AddMemory` and count filtering, with a small test surface.
commented.api_surface: No exported API change is needed; this is backend behavior under the existing memory service contract.
commented.change_inventory: Adds a GORM memory backend, models, filters, and an example module demonstrating SQL-backed memory storage.
commented.semantic_changes: Introduces persistent SQL-backed memory operations with configured limits and soft delete semantics.
commented.module_impact: Affects `memory/gorm` users who configure a low or exact memory limit and update or replace existing memories.
commented.cross_module_impact: The example module depends on the backend behavior; other memory backends are not changed.
commented.behavior_impact: At capacity, duplicate or replacement writes can fail incorrectly, and deleting a memory may not allow adding a new active memory.
commented.reproduction_on_base: A temporary focused test showed both failure modes: re-adding the same memory at limit returned a limit error, and adding a new memory after soft delete remained blocked.
commented.issue_evidence_gaps: The desired semantics are strongly implied by generated memory identity and soft delete behavior; no broader API decision appears necessary.
commented.tests_docs: `go test ./...` passed in `memory/gorm`; `go test ./...` passed in `examples/memory/gorm`. The focused reproducer failed before the fix, as expected.
commented.outcome: Submitted a COMMENT review with one inline blocker; no approval, LGTM, or merge action was taken.
commented.ci_state: All effective checks were green in the final snapshot.
commented.risk: Without the fix, production users can hit permanent capacity errors even when performing idempotent updates or after deleting existing active memories.
commented.merge_policy: Comment-only run; external contributor PR also requires explicit maintainer LGTM before merge.

inline_comments.summary: Limit enforcement happens before canonical memory identity and counts soft-deleted rows, so idempotent updates and post-delete inserts are rejected at capacity.

follow_up.title: Ready PR queue needs explicit human action
follow_up.reason: Several green PRs are held by external-contributor or comment-only policy rather than code findings.
follow_up.next: Maintainers should decide which ready PRs receive explicit LGTM after the contributor-program review batch is complete.

follow_up.title: Own PR merge-conflict maintenance path
follow_up.reason: The workflow should repair WineChord-owned dirty merge states in a temporary worktree when the user allows maintenance actions.
follow_up.next: Add a guarded maintenance step that fetches base, attempts a no-commit merge or rebase in an isolated worktree, runs focused tests, and pushes only when clean.

follow_up.title: Report completeness guard
follow_up.reason: A PR head moved during the run, so the initial checks snapshot became stale for that PR.
follow_up.next: Keep the final checks refresh as a required report-generation input and fail report generation when final PR heads differ from the checks snapshot.

timeline.detail: `git pull --ff-only` reported already up to date.
Final open PR count: 55; delta from previous report: new 3, closed 4, head-changed 9.
Focused review and targeted tests were performed for #2151, #2128, #2125, #2141, #2146, and #2134; only #2151 and #2128 produced new blockers.
Submitted COMMENT reviews on #2151 and #2128. No LGTM, approval, explicit merge, or self-review was performed.
Skipped groups cover 53 PRs, and the report coverage record covers all 55 open PRs for the next review run.

skill_evolution.note: A PR head changed after the initial collection; future full runs should refresh checks immediately before report generation and compare head SHAs before classifying readiness.
Every open PR should appear exactly once in processed or skipped coverage, with item-level readiness audit and concrete CI/review blockers.
When the user requests comments only, ready PRs should be visibly separated from CI/review-blocked PRs so the HTML does not look incomplete.
Design a guarded path for WineChord-owned merge conflicts: isolated worktree, base reconciliation, focused tests, and no self-review action.
